steps.title: Identify the parallel list and its possessive forms
steps.content: The sentence lists three elements: “from the territory's history to ______ architecture to its coffee.” The first uses “the territory's” (singular possessive) and the last uses “its” (singular possessive). Both refer to Puerto Rico, which is singular. The blank must follow the same pattern.

steps.title: Apply the singular possessive to the blank
steps.content: ”Its” is the singular possessive pronoun that fits the blank, maintaining the pattern of singular possessive forms. The other choices don't work: they're and it's are contractions lacking possessive meaning, and their is plural. That matches choice A — A is correct.
